articles.summary: Several cases of settler violence during first day of eid, mother and child beaten Israeli settlers on Wednesday raided a number of areas across the West Bank, beating residents and animals as well as destroying Palestinian properties.  One viral video showed an Israeli settler attacking a dog belonging to a Palestinian family in Masafer Yatta, Hebron. articles.title: War in the Middle East 'reshaping' energy investment strategies globally: IEA
articles.summary: War in the Middle East 'reshaping' energy investment strategies globally: IEA The conflict in the Middle East is pushing governments to find new supply routes and turn to domestic resources to brave the world's biggest energy crisis, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said Thursday. "We are in the midst of the largest energy security crisis the world has ever faced... and I believe this will reshape investment strategies globally, with parallels to the major changes the energy world witnessed after the oil shocks of the 1970s," said IEA executive director Fatih Birol. articles.title: Trump threatens to 'blow up' Oman if it joins Iran in imposing fees on Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: Trump threatens to 'blow up' Oman if it joins Iran in imposing fees on Hormuz Strait US President Donald Trump threatened to “blow up” Oman if it agreed to work with Iran to impose a fee for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz as part of a settlement to end the war on Tehran. “Oman will behave just like everybody else, or we’ll have to blow them up.” US President Donald Trump threatened to “blow up” Oman if it agreed to work with Iran to impose a fee for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz as part of a settlement to end the war on Tehran pic.twitter.com/sv1KeL67ch — Middle East Eye (@Middl

articles.summary: Copper extended losses alongside other industrial metals after the US carried out fresh strikes in the Persian Gulf and a deal to end the war with Iran remained elusive.

articles.title: IRGC claims attack on US base in retaliation to Washington's strikes near Bandar Abbas
articles.summary: IRGC claims attack on US base in retaliation to Washington's strikes near Bandar Abbas Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said they targeted a US air base at 4.50am local time (1.50am GMT) in response to Washington's attack near Bandar Abbas airport, the Tasnim news agency reported. They vowed to take on a "more decisive" response if there is another attack, adding that the responsibility for the consequences lay with the "aggressor". The IRGC did not specify where the base is located.

articles.title: Iran fired at four ships attempting to pass through Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: Iran fired at four ships attempting to pass through Hormuz Strait Iranian forces fired at four vessels attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz, according to state broadcaster IRIB. "Four vessels attempted to cross the Strait of Hormuz and enter the Persian Gulf without coordination with the security forces," IRIB stated, but did not provide any additional details about the ships.  "They were warned, but after they ignored the warning, warning shots were fired at them, forcing them to return," it added.

This comes as Iranian parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has repeatedly insisted that unlocking access to his country's money should be a confidence-building measure, if not a precondition, for talks with the US.  Both Ghalibaf and Iran’s central bank chief travelled to Qatar on M

articles.title: US carries out new hits on Iranian military sites: Report
articles.summary: US carries out new hits on Iranian military sites: Report The US military has launched attacks on Iran targeting an army base that Washington officials allege poses a threat to its troops and commercial maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, according to a US official speaking to Reuters on Wednesday. The official added that the US army also struck down several Iranian drones. Iran’s Fars news agency earlier reported three explosions that were heard east of the port city of Bandar Abbas.

articles.title: South Korea summons Iranian ambassador over attack on vessel
articles.summary: South Korea summons Iranian ambassador over attack on vessel South Korea’s Foreign Ministry has summoned Iranian Ambassador Saeed Koozechi to lodge what it described as a strong protest over an attack on a South Korean vessel, according to Yonhap news agency. Seoul demanded “responsible measures, including steps to prevent a recurrence”, the report said. Tensions escalated after South Korea’s Foreign Ministry said an Iranian-made antiship missile was “highly likely” used in an earlier attack on the HMM Namu, operated by South Korean shipping company HMM. articles.title: Death toll rises after Israeli strike on Gaza City home
articles.summary: Death toll rises after Israeli strike on Gaza City home The death toll from an Israeli air strike on a home in central Gaza City has risen to seven, with at least 15 others wounded, according to medical sources at al-Shifa Hospital cited by the Wafa news agency. The strike targeted a house near al-Israa Tower on Omar al-Mukhtar Street, close to the municipal park in the city centre, the report said. According to Wafa, an Israeli warplane fired two missiles at the building.

articles.title: PacSun 'Co-Creating' Brand to Reach Gen Z
articles.summary: PacSun CEO Brieane Olson said that the retailer has built a resurgence by 'co-creating' and listening to a live feedback loop of what Gen Z consumers want to see from the retailer instead of treating young people like a static audience. She said brands have to shit the notion of control and relinquish it some to gain more 'brand and community trust.' (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iran reopens Isfahan airport after more than 80 days
articles.summary: Iran reopens Isfahan airport after more than 80 days Iranian authorities have announced the reopening of Isfahan International Airport, about 440km south of Tehran. According to ISNA news agency, the airport had been closed for more than 80 days during the US-Israeli war on Iran, the governor of Isfahan said. Earlier, Iranian authorities also announced plans to reopen Tabriz International Airport in the country’s northwest after it sustained damage in a US-Israeli attack.
